title: Natural Language Autoencoders Produce Unsupervised Explanations of LLM Activations
raw_content_summary: Academic paper introducing NLAs as an unsupervised method for generating natural language explanations of LLM activations. Architecture: AV (activation verbalizer) + AR (activation reconstructor) trained jointly with RL to minimize MSE reconstruction error. FVE (Fraction of Variance Explained) reaches 0.6-0.8 across models. Five explicit limitations: confabulation, lack of mechanistic grounding, excessive expressivity, cost, degenerate training objective. Applied to pre-deployment audits of Claude Opus 4.6 and Mythos Preview.
